Off Calendar Jun-18-2008 Continued To Jul-02-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-03-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Raymond H. Levy in pro per.

Mr. Levy reported that he has not received any financial information from the bank, and that no information regarding Michael's address had been provided to him. Therefore, he is considering that Michael is still missing, and stated that he was not able to close out the estate without the assets being marshalled. The Court approved the request for an order to obtain financial information from the bank, signing the order the same in open court.
The Court waives notice on Michael and directed Mr. Levy to communicate in writting with the attorney in Ohio for information on Michael.

Judge Pro Tem: Sue Kaplan. Reporter: Giselle Casey CSR#8098. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Larazin.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Dec-17-2007 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES- ATTY. RAYMOND LEVY, EXECUTOR IN PRO PER;
ATTY. JAMES GUCKER APPEARING FOR MARK MASSENGALE.

ATTY. LEVY RAISED A QUESTION WITH REGARD TO A CERTIFICATE OF DEPOSIT. ATTY. GUCKER INFORMED THE COURT THAT MICHAEL WAS REPORTED TO BE STILL ALIVE AND WAS LOCATED IN LAKE TAHOE. MR. LEVY ARGUED THAT SINCE MICHAEL IS NOT LISTED AS BENEFICIARY, HE SHOULD BE DISINHERITED UNTIL LOCATED. MR. LEVY REQUESTED THAT THE COURT ISSUE AN ORDER FOR THE FIRST REPUBLIC BANK TO RELEASE INFORMATION REGARDING DECEDENT'S ACCOUNT AND BENEFICIARY. THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER OFF CALENDAR WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO REFILING THE PETITION. MICHAEL IS ENTITLED TO NOTICE. THE COURT DENIED THE REQUEST OF MR. LEVY TO BE HEARD IN THE EX-PARTE CALENDAR SINCE MICHAEL IS ENTITLED TO NOTICE. MR. GUCKER WILL CONTINUE TO BE IN THE CASE FOR ITS DURATION.

Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: KAREN FARNSWORTH CSR #5162. Clerk: Cynthia Herbert Bailiff: Deputy Leung

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Nov-19-2007 Continued To Dec-17-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Raymond Levy in Pro Per.

The Court questioned the letter received from the attorney in Ohio. Mr. Levy opposed the letter. The Court stated that there was a problem with marshalling assets, and it did not think it had jurisdiction on Pay On Death accounts. The matter was continued to December 17, 2007 to allow the Beneficiary in Ohio to obtain counsel.

Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Mizota.
